title: Concurrency Budgets, Backpressure, Rate Limits, and Worker Threads · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent service team receives a different tenant, traffic, trust, persistence, and failure profile and transfers concurrency budgets, backpressure, rate limits, and worker threads into a new admission, concurrency, queue, rate, and CPU-work budget. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of admission, concurrency, queue, rate, and CPU-work budget with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Distinguish asynchronous concurrency from parallelism and measure CPU blocking, synchronous I/O, long callbacks, and event-loop delay.
Bound active requests, per-dependency concurrency, queue length, queue age, memory, rejection, Retry-After, and fairness separately.
Apply rate and quota policies to trusted identities, tenant and operation cost, distributed storage, clock behavior, headers, exemptions, and failure mode.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Event Loop Lag Blocking
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 5544 in a administrative API crossing two identity and network trust zones. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine concurrency, queue, tenant-rate, and bounded CPU-offload decisions; prove cleanup before the service deadline expires; then change the tenant and prove denial before repository access. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Event Loop Lag Blocking.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish asynchronous concurrency from parallelism and measure cpu blocking, synchronous i/o, long callbacks, and event-loop delay. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Event Loop Lag Blocking, keep Event Loop Lag Blocking connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test saturation, noisy neighbors, dependency slowdown, queue shedding, readiness degradation, recovery time, and invariant preservation.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Overload Recovery Evidence symptom in admission, concurrency, queue, rate, and CPU-work budget during this lab case.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Overload Recovery Evidence
